task: Write a Python function chunk_pad(lst, k) that splits a list into consecutive chunks of size k, in order. When the final chunk comes up short, pad it with None so that every chunk has exactly k elements. An empty list returns []. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function passes all criteria with minor room for improvement in readability.
trace: I first checked for the edge case where the input list is empty or the chunk size `k` is less than or equal to zero. If either condition is met, an empty list is returned. Then, I used a list comprehension to create chunks of the desired size `k`. Finally, if the last chunk has fewer than `k` elements, I padded it with `None` to ensure all chunks are of equal length.
